summary: A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000001) of type Type-A has been filed regarding a transaction between customer John Jensen and counterparty Jeremy Martinez. The transaction involved a cash deposit transfer of NPR 535368.64 on 2022-10-07. The transaction was facilitated through accounts NP00000000003412850188 and NP00000000003980427782 at banks PCBL and SBL, respectively. The countries involved include GB. The transaction has been flagged as suspicious, with the narrative simply stating that a suspicious transaction was observed, providing no additional context or details regarding the nature of the suspicious activity. No other information is available regarding the purpose or origin of the transaction.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000002) was filed for Sally Dominguez, a customer of ADBL, due to a transaction that raised concerns. On 2022-10-07, Dominguez conducted a cash withdrawal transfer of NPR 21,807.13 to Henry King, with account numbers NP00000000000983174071 and NP00000000000894070684 involved. The transaction was routed through HBL and involved countries GB and IN. The amount and velocity of the transaction were deemed inconsistent with Dominguez's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis, as the pattern of transfers appeared to be deliberately kept below reporting thresholds and executed in rapid succession, consistent with cash withdrawal activity. No adverse media or sanctions matches were identified, and the customer has been with the institution for several years.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000003) was filed for customer Cook, Flores and Ray due to a transaction that triggered an internal threshold alert. On 2022-10-07, the customer conducted a cross-border transfer of NPR 1,214,019.81 to counterparty Chad Mccarty, utilizing account numbers NP00000000002968635055 and NP00000000000310823462 at banks KUMARI and SBL. The transaction involved countries GB and MX. The amount and velocity of the transfer were deemed inconsistent with the customer's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis, noting that the customer has been with the institution for some years and no adverse media or sanctions matches were identified, but the pattern of transfers appears to be deliberately kept below reporting thresholds.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000004, Type-B) was filed regarding Robert King's account at PRABHU, which involved a cross-border transfer of NPR 813935.97 to Sarah English on 2022-10-07. The transaction was routed through account numbers NP00000000001187666276 and NP00000000004011650754, and involved banks PRABHU and LAXMI, with countries involved being GB and NG. The transaction mode was a cross-border transfer, and the amount and velocity of the funds were deemed not fully consistent with the customer's declared income profile. The report was submitted for further analysis due to the pattern of transfers, which appeared to be deliberately kept below reporting thresholds and executed in rapid succession. No adverse media or sanctions matches were identified, and the customer has been with the institution for some years.

A Suspicious Transaction Report (RPT-2026-000005) of type Type-A has been filed regarding a transaction involving customer Porter LLC and counterparty Terrence Davis. The transaction occurred on 2022-10-07, with an amount of NPR 1610735.12, using a debit card transfer mode. The transaction involved account numbers NP00000000003628175933 and NP00000000003038338293 at bank MEGA. The countries involved include GB. The narrative provided indicates that a suspicious transaction was observed, but no additional details are available. All relevant information has been included in this summary; no key facts were intentionally excluded.
